supply_chain.dependencies: The Company purchases certain key raw and packaging materials from single-source suppliers or a limited number of suppliers. New suppliers may have to be qualified under industry, governmental and/or Colgate standards, which can require additional investment and/or take a significant period of time.
supply_chain.risks: Disruptions in global supply chain due to geopolitical events (war in Ukraine, conflict in Middle East), strikes and labor disputes, logistics disruptions, loss or impairment of key manufacturing or distribution sites, loss of key suppliers or contract manufacturers, capacity constraints, raw material and product availability and quality issues, industrial accidents, impact on suppliers of tighter credit or capital markets, lack of availability of qualified personnel, governmental regulations and controls (import and export restrictions, tariffs, sanctions, quotas, trade barriers), natural disasters, climatic events, earthquakes, tornadoes, acts of war or terrorism, political unrest, fires, explosions, cybersecurity incidents. Raw and packaging material commodities (resins, essential oils, tropical oils, pulp, tallow, corn, poultry, soybeans) subject to market price variations. Increases in costs of commodities, energy, logistics have affected and may continue to adversely affect profit margins.

regulatory_risks: Extensive legal and regulatory requirements in the U.S. and abroad apply to product development, ingredients, formulation, manufacture, packaging, labeling, storage, transportation, distribution, export, import, advertising, sale and environmental impact

U.S. federal authorities (FDA, Federal Trade Commission, Consumer Product Safety Commission, OSHA, EPA) regulate different aspects of business, along with parallel authorities at state and local levels and comparable authorities overseas
New or more stringent legal or regulatory requirements, or more restrictive interpretations of existing requirements, could adversely impact business

Regulatory authorities review use of various ingredients and packaging content in consumer products - decision that any ingredient or packaging content should be restricted or newly regulated could adversely impact business
Anti-corruption laws and regulations (U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act) prohibit improper payments to foreign government officials and political figures
Laws and sanctions imposed by U.S. (including OFAC) and other jurisdictions may prohibit doing business in certain countries or restrict kind of business conducted

Data protection laws and regulations (General Data Protection Regulation) govern collection, storage, transfer and processing of customer, consumer, employee, vendor and other stakeholder information and personal data
Sustainability, labor and employment practices, artificial intelligence regulations
Extended producer responsibility obligations related to product packaging
Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive disclosure obligations
Climate change regulations (carbon pricing, alternative energy policy, greenhouse gas emissions)
Pillar II Model Rules - minimum corporate tax rate of 15% in every jurisdiction
Trade policies and agreements changes resulting in potentially adverse tax consequences or imposition of trade restrictions, tariffs, sanctions, price controls, profit controls
Competition law regulations on selling practices in U.S. and abroad

sanction_risks: Sanctions imposed by U.S. Treasury Department's Office of Foreign Asset Control (OFAC) and other jurisdictions may prohibit doing business in certain countries
War in Ukraine has resulted in sanctions, export controls and import restrictions
Conflict in Middle East may impact geopolitical relations and stability in North Africa, wider Middle East and nearby regions
Tensions between China and Taiwan may result in trade restrictions and tariffs
New and increased tariffs imposed by United States and other countries (China, Mexico, Canada) following U.S. February 2025 executive orders
Challenges to ability to repatriate cash from Russia and identify banking partners to support Russian operations
May face challenges to ability to protect assets in Russia
